One platform. One price. Student-Centric Filtering The unique design integrates with your Google Admin Console, Active Directory, or SIS and allows the filter to be deployed on or off campus. Granular controls allow for truly customizable YouTube filtering across device types. One size fits all filtering is a thing of the past. In a matter of seconds, ensure proper filtering for all students, regardless of school, grade, or class subject. Asset Utilization & Reporting Geolocate student devices without placing the device in lost or stolen mode. Quickly inventory software, hardware, and web application assets for year-end audits and spending analysis. Student Behavior Analytics Quickly inventory software, hardware, and web application assets, as well as student activity reports. Real-time alerts provide peace of mind that staff are notified of inappropriate or unsafe behavior on school-issued devices. Classroom Management Tools Seamless integration with your Student Information System allows educators to focus on teaching, not adjusting rosters or class schedules. Intuitive classroom technology supports educators, making it easier to monitor student devices and maximize classroom engagement. AristotleK12 Compatibility: Chromebooks Windows Mac iOS Virtual Desktops Printers Why choose AristotleK12? 1. Ultimate Flexibility The needs of educators change fast, and technology changes even faster. AristotleK12 takes the headache out of managing ever-changing requirements and provides the technology needed to quickly adjust system settings to meet any student, educator, or district needs. 2. Privacy & Retention Policy Ensuring student safety and data privacy does not have to be one or the other. Sergeant Laboratories has never and will never view, sell, store, or share student information. We commit to this principle by signing the most restrictive Data Privacy Agreements from school districts and states across the nation. Rest assured that with AristotleK12, your students’ data will never be for sale. 3. Functionality Across Device Types AristotleK12 provides high-end features through a user-friendly interface that is compatible with a variety of device types. 4. All-in-One Value Because AristotleK12 is an all-in-one solution, there are no additional fees for updates to the system. AristotleK12 is everything you need to keep students protected and productive, under one pane of glass, at one affordable price. 5. Unmatched Support and Training Comprehensive training and support are included at no additional cost. Our experienced, US-based support teams are certified and equipped to handle all of your training and support needs. Authorized Data Providers This section allows you to select specific data providers that a district can connect to your application. Clever Integration This section allows you to connect your Edlink application with an existing Clever application. Read more Resources Blog Podcast Product Tours Guides and Toolkits Surveys Webinars Events Find Funding Company About Panorama Our Research In the Press Data Privacy Join Our Team Panorama Website Privacy Policy Thank you for your interest in Panorama Education (“ Panorama ”, “ we ”, “ our ”, or “ us ”). This Website Privacy Policy explains how we gather, use, and share personal information from individuals who access or interact with our company’s website or otherwise communicate with us via our company’s website, email, phone or mail. By accessing or interacting with our company’s website or otherwise communicating with us, you accept the information practices described in this Website Privacy Policy. This Website Privacy Policy does not apply to our handling of students’ and other personal information provided to us as a service provider by schools, districts, local education agencies or other educational institutions that use our technology platform, services and products (“Clients”), or to students’ and other personal information collected by us as a service provider at the direction of Clients. Our handling of such information is governed by our written agreements with our Clients and applicable laws. Please review our Client Information Policy for additional information. If you are a parent, guardian, or community member, please visit Privacy, Safety, and Trust at Panorama Education . Children’s Privacy Protection We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under the age of 18 unless we are performing services for a Client and believe we have appropriate permission to do so, as described in our Client Information Policy . If we become aware or otherwise have reason to believe that we have received personal information of a child under the age of 18 without appropriate permission, we will take prompt steps to remove such information. If you suspect that personal information of a child under the age of 18 has been submitted to us without appropriate permission, please contact us at privacy@panoramaed.com . Information We May Gather and How We May Gather It When You Request Access to Our Website Content and Events . We may request your personal information, such as name, address, telephone number, email address, and school or district information when you access certain content made available on our company website. Likewise, we may request your personal information when you register to attend one of our in-person or virtual events. We may also ask you to create a user account with us if you request access to certain resources made available on our company website. When You Subscribe to Our Email Lists . You may voluntarily subscribe to our email list(s) to stay informed about our company, events, services and products. In addition to your email address, we may request you to provide your name and school or district information. To be removed from our list, you can click on the “unsubscribe” link in one of our email messages or send your request via email to privacy@panoramaed.com with “REMOVE ME” in the subject line. Please note that your opt-out will apply to communications that are primarily of a marketing nature; we may still send you communications if necessary to respond to your inquiries or requests or provide you with important notices regarding a service or product you have requested or have received. When Third Parties Share Your Information with Us . We may obtain your personal information from a third party to whom you have given permission to share your personal information. For example, if you subscribe to a resource maintained by a third party and consent to that third party’s sharing your personal information with us, we may receive such information from that third party. We may also obtain information about you from third-party databases or services. We may use this information to improve the accuracy of our records. With Automated Technologies . Just as other similar service providers do, Panorama may gather information that is generated automatically when a user interacts with our company website and email communications, such as IP address, browser type, information about how, how often and when a user engages with our company website and whether an email message from us has been opened. Panorama may gather this information using cookies (small data files on your computer’s browser), web beacons (invisible electronic images embedded on web pages) and similar technologies. In some cases, third parties may deliver cookies to your browser on our behalf. Some of these third party technologies may collect information about your browsing activities over time and across different websites. For example, we use Google Analytics, a web analytics service provided by Google, Inc. (“Google”), which uses cookies to count the number of visitors to our website and how they engage with it (learn more about Google’s practices at https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=en-US ). You can opt out of Google Analytics by downloading and installing the browser plug-in available at: https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout . Panorama is not responsible for the effectiveness of any browser setting or third-party opt-out options, and the functionality of our website may be affected by your settings and opt-out choices. Please note that our website does not respond to “Do Not Track” (“DNT”) signals at this time. If we do respond to DNT signals in the future, we will update this Website Privacy Policy to describe how we do so. How We May Use Information Overall Experience and Support . We may use the information described above to provide you with relevant website content, inform you of service and product offerings, operate and improve our website, and support your overall experience with us. We may use your information to: communicate information and notices relating to your use of our company website, services and products; provide customer service and respond to your inquiries or requests; create a personalized, convenient and enhanced website and visitor experience (for example, designing our website to remember if you have already filled out a form field so you do not need to fill out that field again); provide customized content and inform you of offerings we think may be of interest to you; monitor usage of our website and diagnose and resolve any issues; improve our content and services and the usability of our website; and protect the security and integrity of our website and content. You may opt out of our direct marketing communications by clicking on the “unsubscribe” link in one of our email messages or by sending your request via email to privacy@panoramaed.com with “REMOVE ME” in the subject line. Please note that your opt-out will apply to communications that are primarily of a marketing nature; we may still send you communications if necessary to respond to your inquiries or requests or provide you with important notices regarding a service or product you have requested or have received. Compliance and Protection . We may use your information to: protect our, your, or others’ rights, privacy, safety or property; audit our internal processes for compliance with legal and contractual requirements and internal policies; prevent, identify, investigate and deter fraudulent, harmful, unauthorized, unethical or illegal activity, including cyberattacks and identity theft; and comply with applicable laws, lawful requests, and legal process, such as to respond to subpoenas or requests from government authorities. How We May Share Information We do not sell, rent or market your personal information to other companies without permission. We may share your personal information as follows: With Our Service Providers . When necessary, we may disclose personal information to a third party that helps us operate our company website or facilitates the activities described in this Website Privacy Policy. With Content and Event Partners . When you sign-up with us to access certain content contributed by a third party or register for an event co-organized or involving third parties, we may share your name and email address with such third parties, but only after notifying you and giving you an opportunity to not have your information shared. With Your Permission if You Provide a Testimonial . With your permission, we may display a personal testimonial or endorsement from you about our products and services on our company website and in internal team materials, and we may include your name and school or district if you give us permission. As Required by Law or to Ensure Safety, Security and Integrity . We may be required to disclose or grant access to personal information to law enforcement, government authorities, and other parties in order to comply with law enforcement requests and legal processes. We may also do so to protect users’, our, or others’ rights, property, or safety. As Part of a Business Transition. ​​​​In the event Panorama goes through a business transition, such as a merger with, acquisition by or sale of all or a portion of its assets to another company, or other corporate change, your personal information may be disclosed or transferred to the other company and other relevant parties as part of the process in accordance with applicable law. Any successor entity would be required to comply with applicable law in its handling of personal information. How We Protect Information We are committed to protecting the personal information we gather. We implement administrative, technical, and physical security procedures reasonably designed to protect such information. We use security safeguards such as physical access controls to buildings and files, data encryption, Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) cryptography, two-factor authentication, and firewalls to help prevent unauthorized access to the personal information we maintain. Please note that even with safeguards, neither the Internet nor any data storage system can be guaranteed to be 100% secure. We keep your personal information for only as long as necessary for the purposes described in this Website Privacy Policy. We may also retain your personal information to comply with our tax, accounting and financial reporting obligations and as otherwise required by law. Additional Information International Use. Panorama is based in the United States and its website, communications, services and products are intended primarily for use within the United States. pages.text: Privacy | ART Math top of page Skip to Main Content Product Privacy About Us More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Book a Demo Privacy Policy Last updated: March 2026 1. Overview ArtMath® ("ArtMath", "we", "our", or "us") is committed to protecting the privacy of students, teachers, and school administrators who use our platform. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the choices you have regarding your data. ArtMath ® is designed for use in K-8 educational settings. We take extra care to comply with the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA), the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA), and applicable state student data privacy laws. 2. Information We Collect We collect only the information necessary to deliver and improve the ArtMath ® platform. ​ Account information: Name, email address, and role (teacher, student, or administrator) provided at registration. Student work: Drawings, written responses, and submissions created by students within the platform. Usage data: Interaction logs, feature usage, and performance data used to improve the platform. Device information: Browser type, operating system, and IP address for security and compatibility purposes. ​​ We do not collect sensitive personal information such as Social Security numbers, financial data, or health information. 3. How We Use Your Information Information collected is used solely to: ​ Provide and operate the ArtMath ® platform for teachers and students Generate real-time insights and feedback on student work Support teachers with class-level and individual-level analytics and misconception tracking Improve platform features, reliability, and performance Respond to support requests and communicate service updates We do not use student data to build profiles for advertising purposes, and we do not sell or rent any personal information to third parties. 4. Student Data & FERPA Compliance ArtMath ® operates as a "school official" under FERPA, meaning we access student education records only to perform services on behalf of the school or district. All student data remains the property of the school or district. ​ Student data is never sold, shared, or used for advertising Access to student records is limited to authorized teachers and administrators Schools and districts retain full control over their data and may request deletion at any time 5. Children's Privacy & COPPA Compliance ArtMath ® is designed for use by children under 13 in school settings. We comply with COPPA by: ​ Collecting only the minimum data necessary for educational use Obtaining consent through the school or district, rather than directly from children Never using children's data for advertising or behavioural profiling Providing parents and guardians the right to review, correct, or delete their child's data 6. Data Security We use industry-standard security measures to protect all data on the ArtMath ® platform:​ ​ All data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest (AES-256) Access to production systems is restricted to authorized personnel only We conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments In the event of a data breach, we will notify affected schools and districts promptly in accordance with applicable law 7. Data Retention & Deletion We retain student and teacher data only for as long as needed to provide the service or as required by the school's agreement with ArtMath ® . Schools and districts may request deletion of their data at any time by contacting us. Upon account termination, data is deleted within 30 days unless a longer retention period is required by law. 8. Third-Party Services ArtMath ® may use trusted third-party service providers (such as cloud hosting and analytics) to operate the platform. These providers are contractually bound to process data only on our behalf and in accordance with this Privacy Policy. We do not share personal information with third parties for their own marketing purposes. 9. Your Rights Depending on your location, you may have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. Teachers and administrators can manage their account data directly in the platform. For student data, please contact your school or district administrator, or reach out to us directly. 10. Changes to This Policy We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will notify schools and districts via email and post an updated version on this page. Continued use of ArtMath ® after changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated policy. 11. Contact Us If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please contact us: ArtMath ® ​ Email: ContactUs@artmath.org ​ Website: artmath.org Your math teaching partner for revealing students' strengths and gaps in real time. pages.text: Curipod - Writing Practice Students Love Korean (South Korea) 로그인 무료 가입 쓰기 연습 학생들이 사랑하는 교사 주도 수업이 귀 학교 교과 과정과 일치합니다. 학생들은 읽고, 쓰고, 토론합니다. 혼자가 아닌 화면 속에서. 함께. 가입하기 - 무료 데모 예약 14,000개 이상의 학군에서 신뢰받음 Students Scored 23 Points Higher on State Tests 50% Baseline 73% With Curipod 주 평가에서 입증된 실제 성과 Curipod 교실의 학생들은 주 읽기 및 쓰기 시험에서 최대 23%p 향상했으며, 가장 뒤처졌던 학생들이 가장 큰 성장을 보였습니다. 캘리포니아 교육부와 텍사스 교육청의 공식 주 시험 데이터에 기반한 사례 연구 연구 보기 → 교과 과정 에 몰입 과 글쓰기 제공 HMH Into Reading, Amplify CKLA 및 이미 학군에서 사용 중인 다른 HQIM 과 함께 1 단계와 2 단계 수업에 Curipod 를 사용하세요 지원되는 커리큘럼 보기 → 구체적이며 실행 가능한 실시간 AI 피드백 학생들은 제출하는 즉시 피드백 을 받습니다. 읽고, 토론하고, 다시 시도합니다. 모두 같은 수업 시간 안에, 그리고 Hattie 의 연구에 맞추어. 데모 예약 → 주요 오해 학생들은 근거 찾기 텍스트에서 구체적인 세부 정보 대신 요약 문장을 인용하여 3 학생들이 도움이 필요해요 후속 조치 대상으로 표시됨 MR JT SK 모든 수업 에서 오는 보고서 및 인사이트 Curipod 는 교실 활동을 실행 가능한 보고서로 전환합니다 . 모든 응답, 성찰, 수정이 증거가 되어 학습 격차를 파악하고 조정하며 수업을 개별화할 수 있습니다. 모든 목소리 가 들려요 학습은 사회적입니다 항상 그랬습니다 Curipod 는 그것을 중심으로 구축되었습니다 교사가 이끄는 전체 학급 수업에서 모든 학생이 함께 읽고, 쓰고, 토론합니다 고개를 숙이고 화면에 홀로 있는 것이 아닙니다 소리 내어, 대화로, 당신이 퍼실리테이터로서 수업 진행 모습 보기 → 교사 및 학교 지도자들이 Curipod 를 사랑하는 이유 55개 이상의 평가가 높은 커리큘럼과 일치함 Curipod 는 이미 신뢰받는 학교의 교육과정에 맞추어 매핑되며, 우리는 학군과 직접 협력하여 그들의 범위, 순서, 학생들에 맞는 수업 세트를 구축합니다 Dickinson ISD 가 Curipod 를 Bluebonnet 와 함께 활용하는 방법 읽기 → 이미 사용 중인 교육과정과 정합됨 Bluebonnet Learning CKLA Amplify SAVVAS myPerspectives HMH Into Reading EL Education Wit & Wisdom StudySync myView Literacy Benchmark Advance ARC Core Into Literature Collections ReadyGEN SpringBoard ELA 그리고 40+ 더 더 보기 평가자가 점수 매기는 항목 Danielson FFT · 2022 에디션 2 도메인 2 · 학습 환경 2b 학습을 위한 문화 조성 몇몇 손만 드는 게 아니라 모든 학생이 응답 3 도메인 3 · 학습 경험 3b Engaging Students in Learning Active intellectual work, not passive listening. 3c Communicating About Purpose & Content Clear learning intentions, every slide. 3d Using Assessment for Learning Real-time evidence; teachers adjust on the fly. 3e Responsiveness & Flexibility Pivots based on what students actually show. 강력한 수업을 모든 교사가 활용할 수 있도록 Curipod makes it possible for every teacher to deliver lessons full of engagement, discussion, and writing. Built on the science of writing from Dr. Hochman's The Writing Revolution, Danielson, Marzano, T-TESS, CSTP, and state rubrics. 처음부터 설계된 개인 정보 보호 및 보안 Curipod 는 학교를 위해 만들어졌습니다 — FERPA, COPPA, 그리고 GDPR 준수. 학생들은 계정을 만들지 않습니다. pages.text: The Curipod + Texas Bluebonnet Curriculum English (US) Log in Sign Up For Free Bluebonnet is the curriculum. Curipod is the engagement and insights layer. Student engagement. Rigorous writing practice. Clarity and insights district leaders need. Book a Demo See District Success Story → Students Engaged and Writing During Every Read-Aloud AI-scored SCRs + ECRs, STAAR-aligned. No stack of papers to grade. Built into the Bluebonnet read-aloud. No extra minutes, no extra prep. Bluebonnet questions, feedback in seconds. Not next week. Connect with our team to learn how Curipod maps into your Bluebonnet scope & sequence. Book a Demo Grade 3 · Unit 1 · Lesson 5 — Read-Aloud from The Wind in the Willows Language, Grammar, and Phonics Practice (K-5) The practice, feedback, and mastery loop students need before the spiral moves on. Fun, meaningful phonics practice where students want to write. Sentence-level grammar practice applied in context, not drilled in isolation. Insights for your Bluebonnet implementation See where mastery is, and isn't. Spot the gap before STAAR does. Built for Texas accountability alignment: HB 1605 & HB 8. Interested in seeing your district's dashboard? Contact our team Built for Your Pacing Guide Questions, scaffolds, and student exemplars embedded in lessons. Timed activities keep classes moving forward together. Feedback and revision cycles that used to take hours happen in minutes. Emergent Bilingual Support Built-in translation support. Multilingual response options. Scaffolding built into each activity so every EB student participates. How Dickinson ISD is bringing Bluebonnet to life. Dickinson ISD Region 4 Galveston County, TX K-5 Bluebonnet RLA The partnership Dickinson ISD is adopting Bluebonnet Learning RLA for the 2026-27 school year, and they wanted the rollout to land with real energy in classrooms. Jennifer Brewer, Dickinson's K-12 Literacy Coordinator, and her literacy team knew the curriculum was strong, but they wanted something more: an engagement and insights layer that would make Bluebonnet come alive in classrooms. So she reached out to Curipod, already implemented at the high school level, to build it together. We jumped in side by side. The two teams sat down with the curriculum unit by unit. We met with teachers and coaches. We watched lessons unfold in dozens of real classrooms. And the opportunities came into focus fast: pacing, insights, writing practice with real-time feedback, grammar and skill practice, phonics, and Spanish support. We agreed on where Curipod could add the most value, and got to work. What it looks like in Dickinson From there, the Curipod team built lesson structures, alignments, and insights reporting. First for Dickinson teachers, and ultimately for teachers across Texas. Every activity was tested in multiple classrooms, with multiple teachers, until we knew it actually worked. And then the district saw what happens when Curipod is embedded inside Bluebonnet. Kids leaned in. Wrote more. Asked sharper questions. Stayed with hard ideas longer than anyone expected. Together with Dickinson, we've built a growing library of Curipod activity formats: SCRs, ECRs, phonics, grammar, skill practice. Designed to fit the rhythm of every Bluebonnet K-5 lesson. Ready for the 2026-27 school year. What I love about working with Curipod is they don't hand you a finished product. They sit down with you, and you build it together. My teachers feel ownership of it, and I know that means it will make a real impact. Jennifer Brewer, K-12 Literacy Coordinator, Dickinson ISD Real STAAR gains across Texas — now mapped to every Bluebonnet lesson. Districts already use Curipod to move STAAR scores, campus ratings, and passing rates. These are results your Bluebonnet implementation can build on. 69% → 89% Green Valley's Third-Grade Growth Story. pages.title: Inteliflow — Learning Intelligence. Built on pedagogy. Powered by AI.
pages.text: Inteliflow — Learning Intelligence. Built on pedagogy. Powered by AI. Skip to content Problem Solution Products Founders Compliance Forms Contact CORE SPARK LIFT PULSE Schedule a Demo Menu THE GPS OF MODERN LEARNING Learning Intelligence. Built on pedagogy. Powered by AI. Every student learns differently. Until now, schools had no scalable way to see it. Inteliflow changes that. By combining learning insight, instructional personalization, and real-time student support, Inteliflow helps schools move from reactive education to responsive education. Four connected platforms. One revolutionary learning ecosystem. Explore LIFT Explore CORE Explore SPARK Explore PULSE Inteliflow isn’t a SIS or an LMS. It’s the intelligence layer that sits on top of the systems your school already runs — no rip-and-replace. See where we fit ↓ The Problem Every Day, Schools Make High-Stakes Decisions About Learning — Most of Them in the Dark Schools know a student's grades, attendance, and test scores. What they rarely know is how that student actually processes information — and that's the thing that determines whether the support a school designs works, whether a lesson lands, whether curiosity takes root, and whether the strategy a teacher built actually reaches the classroom that needs it. Placement and onboarding happen before anyone has a clear picture of how the student learns — so consequential decisions get made on records, not readiness. Teachers are asked to personalize for twenty-five learners at once, with no practical way to see who understood, who's ready to move, and who needs a different way in. And even when the right instructional strategy exists, it too often fails to reach every classroom — consistently, equitably, reliably. No usage minimums No weekly minute targets. No time-on-task leaderboards. No teacher evaluations tied to platform compliance. Inteliflow is a teaching tool, not a usage-compliance regime. The platform earns its place in the classroom by being useful — not by enforcing minutes. Who this is for Built for schools that want more than one-size-fits-all learning Independent Schools Thoughtful learning differentiation and admissions insight. School Districts Scalable personalization without adding teacher overload. International Schools Future-ready learning with strong privacy and implementation flexibility. EdTech Partners A connected layer that expands learning insight across programs and products. Before / after From Fragmented Data to Connected Learning Intelligence Schools don't need another tool stacked on top of the last one. They need a system where insight flows forward — from arrival, through daily instruction, into hands-on discovery, and into every classroom that depends on it. Decisions made on records alone → Learning insight on day one One-size-fits-all instruction → Differentiated response built from real comprehension Passive content consumption → Active discovery that builds lasting understanding Inconsistent classroom delivery → Reliable learning experiences in every classroom The CORE Moment — where the ecosystem becomes teaching action Where Learning Insight Becomes a Teaching Decision 1 A student begins to lose the thread in a lesson. 2 CORE notices where the comprehension is breaking down — not two weeks from now, but right now. 3 The teacher gets a clear read on what kind of support this student actually needs. 4 Instruction adjusts before frustration becomes failure. Meet Inteliflow A Connected System Designed for Real School Needs Inteliflow is designed as a connected system that helps schools understand, support, and guide learning across the entire student journey. Insight LIFT Reveal how each student learns — before instruction even begins. Learning CORE Personalized and differentiated learning in real time based on comprehension and learning style. Discovery SPARK Extend learning into real-world challenges, exploration, and critical thinking. Support PULSE Help schools deliver learning more reliably across the whole school environment. What we measure Effort. Consistency. Trajectory. Not just right or wrong. Most ed-tech tools measure correctness and call it learning. Inteliflow measures how a student engages with their work over time — the dimensions that actually predict growth and that teachers can act on. Effort How a student approached the work — independent success, effortful success, struggling-but-trying, or first-time-asking-for-help. Effort is the leading indicator we surface to teachers, parents, and (especially) the student. Consistency Day-over-day, week-over-week. Is this student showing up steadily? Is their participation building? Consistency is the foundation of learning — not test scores frozen in time. Trajectory Where the student was last month, where they are this week. We measure direction, not position. A student rising slowly through difficulty matters more than a student plateaued at advanced. Products One connected platform. Four supportive layers. Each product has its own role, but together they create a connected learning ecosystem built for schools. CORE SPARK LIFT PULSE Learning Insight for Transitions Problem LIFT helps schools understand how students learn before they arrive, strengthening placement, preparation, and transitions. Why Current Solutions Fall Short Most current solutions focus on records, forms, or static measures. They rarely surface learning style preferences, comprehension patterns, and approach to learning tasks in a way schools can use right away. Solution Students complete a short learning insight experience. LIFT identifies learning style preferences, comprehension patterns, and approach to learning tasks, then gives schools a clear profile to inform admissions, onboarding, and student support planning. Impact Smarter onboarding decisions Stronger first-week fit Better student support planning Explore LIFT Classroom Learning Support Problem CORE turns learning insight into everyday teaching. Teachers upload a lesson and CORE helps turn it into differentiated instruction. Why Current Solutions Fall Short Most tools organize content or measure outcomes after the fact. They do not identify comprehension level and learning style preference in a way that helps teachers respond right away. Solution CORE generates personalized quizzes, identifies comprehension level and learning style, and creates differentiated assignments aligned with proven learning strategies and critical thinking frameworks. Impact Personalized assignments automatically generated Instant insight into student learning needs Clear differentiated pathways for every learner Explore CORE Hands-On Interdisciplinary Challenges Problem Students often struggle to connect learning to real-world thinking, challenges, and discovery. Why Current Solutions Fall Short Traditional systems focus on instruction delivery but often do not build curiosity, iteration, and applied thinking in meaningful ways. Solution SPARK creates guided experiential learning where students explore, take on challenges, and generate insight through interaction with AI and real-world scenarios. Impact Higher engagement Stronger critical thinking Future-ready learners Explore SPARK Reliable Access to Learning Resources Problem Schools need dependable access to rich learning experiences across classrooms, even when delivery conditions are not ideal. Why Current Solutions Fall Short Most digital systems assume ideal connectivity and consistent infrastructure. That can leave schools exposed to interruptions, uneven implementation, and delivery gaps. Solution PULSE protects continuity by helping schools distribute learning resources more reliably and more equitably across real school environments. Impact Greater reliability Better continuity More equitable delivery Explore PULSE Where we fit Your SIS stores the student. Your LMS delivers the lesson. Neither knows who will thrive. Schools already have a system of record and a way to deliver content. What they’ve never had is a layer that understands how each student actually learns — and helps you act on it. Inteliflow doesn’t replace your SIS or your LMS. It makes them smarter. Infrastructure — what you already run SIS System of record PowerSchool · Blackbaud · Veracross · Infinite Campus LMS Content delivery Canvas · Schoology · Google Classroom · Moodle Intelligence — what Inteliflow adds LIFT Admissions Admissions intelligence CORE Classroom Learning intelligence Where should you start? Pick your role: Admissions Curriculum Head of School You'll want both. LIFT tells you who will thrive; CORE shows you how they learn once they're here — one intelligence layer across the whole student journey. Across the student journey Apply SIS / CRM → Evaluate LIFT → Enroll SIS → Learn CORE We sit on top of what you already run. LIFT integrates with your SIS and admissions CRM; CORE syncs grades to your LMS. No rip-and-replace. Explore LIFT Explore CORE Beyond the screen Two structural answers to the screen-time critique. Inteliflow is not built around maximizing time on a tablet. PULSE delivers lessons through the teacher in the classroom; SPARK pushes work into hands-on, real-world challenges. Both are designed so learning keeps happening when the screen is off. PULSE — Edge delivery, classroom-paced Lessons delivered to every classroom through a local edge node — not streamed individually to each student device. Teachers pace the room, not the screen. Reliable in low-bandwidth environments. SPARK — Hands-on, real-world challenges Students become epidemiologists, composers, policy advisors. Thirteen subject domains, software-first with optional hardware. Inquiry and doing, not screen time. Implementation without disruption Powerful for schools. Practical for teachers. Designed to fit into your school — not disrupt it. Works with existing curriculum and teaching workflows. Teacher-friendly onboarding, not another complex system to manage. Pilot in weeks, not months. Designed to scale from one grade, class, or school to a full implementation. Why this matters Schools rarely say no to innovation because they do not care. They say no because they fear disruption. Inteliflow is designed to help lower that barrier. Built for teachers, not just technology Less guesswork. More clarity. Better outcomes. The goal is not to replace the teacher. The goal is to give teachers a better map — so they can respond faster, personalize more effectively, and feel more confident in what to do next. So teachers can focus on teaching, not figuring out what to do next. Leadership Built by Educators. Designed for Impact. Inteliflow was built from real classroom challenges — combining deep educational experience with scalable technology systems. Barbara Leventhal Co-Founder & Chief Education Officer Veteran educator, literacy specialist, and curriculum leader with decades of experience in pedagogy, professional development, and student transformation. Barbara ensures Inteliflow is grounded in real classroom impact, supporting teachers and helping unlock student potential at scale. Marvin Leventhal Founder & Strategic Vision Lead Entrepreneur and education innovator with over 30 years building technology and media platforms. Marvin drives Inteliflow’s vision, market strategy, and global expansion, helping bring pedagogy-first learning support to schools worldwide. Institutional trust What adoption looks like inside a real school Inteliflow is designed to feel ambitious in vision, but practical in implementation. Schools do not need to overhaul everything to begin seeing value. How schools implement this Start where the school's most pressing question lives. Some schools begin with an incoming cohort through LIFT. Others start with a single grade or subject through CORE, an innovation initiative through SPARK, or a district-wide delivery pilot through PULSE. Inteliflow fits existing practice rather than forcing a disruptive change in workflow. W

pages.meta: Kira is the first AI platform that delivers personalized education at scale. Use Kira alongside your existing tools, or as your complete AI-powered teaching solution Kira is the first AI platform that delivers personalized education at scale. Use Kira alongside your existing tools, or as your comple
pages.text: Kira The AI operating system for education Kira runs instruction, assessment, and intervention as one connected system, replacing the tool sprawl that keeps educators from focusing on what actually works. Preview Kira 2.0 Book a demo Schools do not need more AI tools. They need a system. Today, education runs on disconnected software. One tool plans lessons. Another grades. Another tracks interventions. Teachers do the integration manually, and leaders only see results at the end. That’s not a system. It’s a workaround. Kira replaces the workaround. Standards become the backbone. Student work becomes the signal. AI becomes the thing that finally makes them talk to each other. Kira replaces fragmentation with a single learning system. Coherent instruction, not fragmented workflows Kira connects planning, teaching, assessment, and insight into one continuous loop, eliminating handoffs between tools and teams. Real-time visibility into learning Student work becomes evidence immediately. Mastery updates continuously, not after benchmark season. Built-in differentiation at scale Instruction adapts for diverse learners, IEPs, ELLs, interventions, without requiring teachers to recreate materials or manage parallel systems. Fewer tools, lower operational overhead Districts reduce redundant subscriptions while gaining a single source of truth for instruction and learning. One system for every instructional reality Kira adapts to how schools actually operate, all without the need for separate tools, products, or workflows. Curriculum and course creation Generate full, standards-aligned courses for any subject or grade-level in minutes. Go beyond lesson drafts and create lessons that are ready to run. Assessment and mastery Every student response updates a living mastery model that drives grading, feedback, and what happens next. Intervention and MTSS Identify gaps instantly and automate targeted support inside instruction before students fall behind. Multilingual and special populations Deliver rigorous instruction that adapts to each learner’s language proficiency and unique needs, without lowering expectations. Career and workforce pathways Open doors to modern CTE and AI pathways that prepare students for the world they’re actually entering. Flexible and alternative learning models Keep learning moving forward across virtual programs, credit recovery, and staffing gaps, so students never lose momentum. Built for schools. Compliant by design. Teachers review and control all instructional content Rubrics, logic, and adaptations are visible and explainable Student data remains under district control Designed to meet FERPA and COPPA requirements by default Security and privacy you can trust Works with what you already use Kira operates alongside existing LMSs, curriculum, and district infrastructure today, and can absorb more of the stack over time. See how Kira 2.0 works If you’re evaluating AI for real, long-term instructional impact, not experiments, this is where to start. Exploring New Horizons with PTKLF 2024: Empowering Early Childhood Learners The recently updated California Preschool/Transitional Kindergarten Learning Foundations (PTKLF) aims to provide high-quality, equitable, and responsive early childhood education for 3 to 5.5 year olds. The enhancements foster an inclusive framework that integrates advanced child development insights, supports multilingual learners, and accommodates children with diverse abilities. Emphasis on Culturally Responsive Practices Enhances teaching to reflect and incorporate the diverse cultural, racial, and linguistic backgrounds of students, fostering a deeper understanding and respect. Inclusion of Children with Different Abilities Provides broader support and resources for inclusive education, accommodating learners with diverse needs to ensure equitable access for all. Focus on Multilingual Learners Strengthens programs that support home languages and English learning, recognizing multilingual skills as essential cognitive and cultural assets. Expanded Learning Domains Introduces ‘Approaches to Learning’ domain and refines existing ones, allowing for interconnected learning experiences that support holistic development across various areas. Comprehensive Standards Update Introduces 45 new standards in areas like science, math, and social-emotional learning to provide a comprehensive and interdisciplinary journey. Smooth Kindergarten Transition The updates smooth the transition to kindergarten, aligning with common core standards to ensure continuous educational progress. Full PTKLF Standards Alignment Our curriculum is natively designed to align with current PTKLF standards through a cohesive, 8-unit integrated structure. The core program provides a seamless learning journey across standards, giving educators a complete curriculum experience without extra mapping or patchwork. Children move from exploration to practical application through hands-on, play-based activities that foster cultural awareness, scientific inquiry, and problem-solving skills. Across the integrated units, children explore how the world works—from colors and sounds to energy use in the environment—and engage in community-based projects that connect classroom learning to real-world experiences. This integrated approach helps educators address PTKLF standards within everyday instruction while delivering a high-quality, engaging learning experience across the full curriculum. We develop personalized UDL and CLR strategies to support IEP and ELD students, fostering a welcoming and inclusive environment for children from diverse cultural backgrounds. pages.meta: The Innovation Institute is a boutique professional learning conference for educators of PK-12 students. The Innovation Institute is a boutique professional learning conference for educators of PK-12 students.
pages.text: Pine Crest School's Innovation Institute 12th Annual Innovation Institute | April 20-21, 2026 2026-04-20 08:15:00 Registration Keynote Accommodations Fueling PK to 12 Curious Minds: Create an Experiential Learning Blueprint Integrating AI, Entrepreneurship, Innovation, VR and More! Join us at Pine Crest School in Fort Lauderdale for the 11th Annual Innovation Institute on Monday, April 7, and Tuesday, April 8, 2025! The cost to attend the Innovation Institute is $525 per ticket. The collection of educators, innovators and experts will provide attendees with meaningful classroom programming they can use when they return to their schools. Led by Keynote Speakers Adam Mangana and Patrick Schuermann, of Optima Independent and Ade Ajayi and Jonnie Green from Reality Labs at Meta, the Institute will offer a variety of teacher-as-student, hands-on workshops geared toward educators at all levels. A ttendees will explore, tinker, meet colleagues from schools from across the country, and leave with techniques and ideas to implement in their own classrooms. Adam and Patrick will lead the opening keynote with a presentation titled "Engagement Elevated: How Immersive Technology Transforms Student Learning." The presentation will dive into how schools can implement these cutting-edge technologies to transform their classrooms and elevate student learning outcomes. By embracing immersive technology, educators can unlock new levels of engagement that promote student success, preparing learners for the complex, technology-driven world of tomorrow. 2025-04-07 09:00:00 Register Today! The countdown is on! We look forward to seeing you in: 2024-04-15 08:00:00 Day 1 Monday Day 2 Tuesday Show All Fort Lauderdale Campus 8:15 - 9:00 a.m. Registration, Continental Breakfast, and Welcome Sign in at the registration desk and collect your resource bag for the event. The conference organizers (Dr. Lisa Ockerman, Vice President of Academics and Strategic Initiatives; Jessie Metzger, Executive Director of Educational and Technology Design and Upper School Dean of Faculty Services; and Dr. Wendy Swanson, Director of Faculty Learning and Recruitment Coordinator) will provide a welcome presentation and overview of Day 1 events for the Innovation Institute. 9:00 – 10:15 a.m. Keynote Generation AI: Preparing Students, Parents, and Educators for a New Era Artificial Intelligence is not just the next big technology; it is the defining force that will shape the lives, careers, and communities of today’s students. For Generation Alpha, the first cohort to grow up never knowing a world without AI, this transformation will redefine education, work and family life. In this keynote, Matt Britton, author of Generation AI , will unpack: The Student Experience : How AI will replace rote memorization with personalized learning and creativity-driven education, while also creating new challenges like shorter attention spans and rising mental health concerns. The Educator’s Role : Why teachers will evolve from knowledge providers to mentors and critical thinking coaches in an AI-enabled classroom. The Parent Perspective : How to guide children in a world where AI shapes their social, political and cultural environments from birth. The World Ahead : What families and schools must do to ensure AI enhances, not diminishes, human potential in the decades to come. Matt Britton Founder and CEO, Suzy 10:15 - 10:30 a.m. Break Pine Crest School Pine Crest neXt 10:30 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. Session 1 A: Build Your Own AI Classroom Assistant Many teachers lose valuable time on routine tasks—writing feedback, summarizing parent emails or generating starter questions—that AI can easily streamline. Google Gems, custom AI assistants within Gemini, capture detailed instructions and workflows as reusable, shareable chatbots. In this 90-minute hands-on workshop, participants progress from exploring Gemini to practicing precise prompting and ultimately build their own Gem—a personal expert agent ready to plan lessons, draft communications, create quizzes and handle everyday classroom workflows. (Repeated Day 2 Session 5A) Kelly Schuster-Paredes Educational AI and Data/Computer Science Technology Specialist Pine Crest School 10:30 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. Session 1 B: The Factory Experience Step into The Factory Experience — Participants will brainstorm and prototype forward-thinking lessons, engaging in a hands-on build using components produced with laser cutters, 3D printers and CNC machines. This creative challenge will demonstrate how digital fabrication tools can bring student ideas to life and be integrated into any curriculum. Join us for a dynamic, design-centered experience that merges imagination, technology and teaching for the learners of tomorrow. Ryan Kesser Upper School Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School Patricia Knight Upper School Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School Mike Stringer Upper School Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School 10:30 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. Session 1C: Building Balance: Understanding and Constructing a Tensegrity Structure In this interactive session, teachers will explore the fascinating architectural principle of tensegrity through a blend of history, theory and hands-on construction. Participants will learn about the origins of tensegrity, its unique balance of tension, compression and its applications in modern design and engineering. Using essential woodworking tools such as a saw, ruler, drill and a doweling jig, teachers will develop practical skills by measuring, cutting, drilling and assembling a wooden desktop tensegrity model with dowel pins, wood glue, and string. This workshop not only reinforces structural concepts but also empowers educators with engaging strategies and tactile experiences to bring complex STEM principles to life in their own classrooms. Rob Bell Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School Alex Vitale Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School 10:30 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. Session 1D: Conceptual Introduction to AI for our Youngest Learners This session provides lower school educators with a practical framework and toolbox of hands-on activities to teach foundational Artificial Intelligence (AI) concepts. Teachers will leave with ready-made lessons that support two key aspects of student AI usage in lower school classrooms: understanding how AI works and using AI tools within the classroom. (Repeated Day 2 Session 5) Amanda Dvorak Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School Erik Swanson Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School 12:00 - 1:00 p.m. Lunch All Innovation Institute attendees will receive a complimentary lunch with their registration. Please notify Meagan Obst (meagan.obst@pinecrest.edu) of any dietary restrictions. Pine Crest School Pine Crest neXt 1:00 - 2:30 p.m. Session 2A: AI in Action: Easy Makerspace Ideas & Activities for Busy Teachers Explore practical strategies for integrating AI into classroom makerspaces with minimal preparation. Participants will leave with ready-to-implement, grade-appropriate project templates that foster creativity, engagement and hands-on learning. Braden Perry Director of Innovation and Entrepreneurship Pine Crest School Vicki Spitalnick Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School 1:00 - 2:30 p.m. Session 2B: Building Proficiency in Coding through Experimentation with Art and Music Students learn to code much like they learn to read and write, do math, draw and play musical instruments. In this hands-on workshop, participants will explore coding with .p5, a simple computer language based on JavaScript. The software .p5 was created for artists and musicians to create interactive experiences. Participants will use a set of tools to improvise with code in a “live” setting - where small code changes can immediately transform the results even when the program is currently running. In this fun and challenging workshop, we will use .p5 to experiment, create and perform for each other. Kris Swanson Director of Educational Technology, Lower and Middle School Robotics, and Esports Upper School Technology Specialist Pine Crest School 1:00 - 2:30 p.m. Session 2C: AR Adventures: Coding Interactive Stories with Ari the Robot What if your students' stories could leap right off the page? Dive into the future of literacy and computer science as we transform classic storytelling into a hands-on, digital adventure! In this session, you'll discover how to merge the physical world of robotics with the magic of Augmented Reality (AR) to create lessons that will captivate your youngest learners. Get ready to design, code and bring a story to life in a way you've never seen before. Marielle Badio Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School Afiha Waite Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School 1:00 - 2:30 p.m. Session 2D: Turning “Cookbook Labs” into Inquiry Activities Are your science labs feeling more like recipes than revelations? This session is designed for educators ready to shift from step-by-step “cookbook” experiments to dynamic, student-driven inquiry. Participants will explore practical strategies to redesign lab activities that foster curiosity, critical thinking and authentic scientific exploration. Wendy Harrigan Upper School Science Department Chair Pine Crest School 2:30 - 4:00 p.m. Session 3A: EDGE Social Entrepreneurship During this session, attendees will learn about Pine Crest School's commitment to social entrepreneurship, reflecting our mission to foster entrepreneurial thinking with a strong emphasis on global collaboration and impact. Attendees will learn about Pine Crest School’s pre-kindergarten through grade twelve social entrepreneurship curriculum. They will learn best practices for fostering intentional communities and collaborative teams among adults, essential for building life-impacting student experiences and programming. Most importantly, participants will hear from students enrolled in the three-year Social Entrepreneurship Fellowship Program as they describe their paths toward creating positive social change. Keri Kolettis Vice President of Global Innovation and Entrepreneurship Pine Crest School Braden Perry Director of Innovation and Entrepreneurship Pine Crest School 2:30 - 4:00 p.m. Session 3B: The Turtle Library in Python Explore creative coding using the Turtle library in Python. Attendees will learn how to use age-appropriate AI tools to create stunning designs. The Turtle library is an excellent tool for understanding fundamental programming concepts and visualizing code execution, particularly for those learning to code. Everyone, from beginners to experienced coders, can join in the experience in this hands-on session. Fernanda Valdivia Immersive Learning Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School Myrielle Badio Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School 2:30 - 4:00 p.m. Session 3C: Integrating Computer Science into the Making Process in the iLab Shift your students from tech consumers to creative problem-solvers. This session offers a look into how our PreK-5 teachers connect computer science with our iLab's engineering process. Attendees will explore and learn how to implement our favorite integrated projects, such as Minecraft and Hummingbird Kits. Participants will walk away with actionable ideas, a fresh perspective on student-led projects and a mini-project of their own. (Repeated Day 2 Session 5) Debra Jacoby Computer Science and Technology Specialist Pine Crest School Bethany Pedraza Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School Alex Vitale Innovation Specialist Pine Crest School 2:30 - 4:00 p.m. Session 3D: Learning Dimensions: A Structure for Teaching with Project-based Learning Learning dimensions help assess and document learning by focusing on the holistic development of skills, knowledge and group collaboration. This hands-on session begins with a set of team challenges for attendees to experience project-based learn
